final_text:
Now I can clearly see the situation. The HTAN Biospecimen schema uses ALL_CAPS_SNAKE_CASE for properties (like `HTAN_BIOSPECIMEN_ID`, `HTAN_PARENT_ID`), while the NF-OSI BiospecimenTemplate uses camelCase (like `individualID`, `tumorType`).

The sample mixes fields from both schemas:
- `HTAN_BIOSPECIMEN_ID` and `HTAN_PARENT_ID` are from HTAN.BiospecimenData-v1.1.0-schema.json (HTAN2 data model, title "Biospecimen", `$id: https://w3id.org/htan/biospecimen`)
- `individualID` and `tumorType` are from NF-OSI's `BiospecimenTemplate.json` (`$id: https://repo-prod.prod.sagebase.org/repo/v1/schema/type/registered/org.synapse.nf-biospecimentemplate`)

## Result: Inconsistent / Mixed-schema sample

**No single schema in scope matches all four fields of the sample.** The input mixes fields from two distinct biospecimen schemas:

### Candidate 1 — HTAN Biospecimen (HTAN2 data model v1.1.0)
- **Identifier:** `https://w3id.org/htan/biospecimen` (title: `Biospecimen`)
- **Source path:** `ncihtan/htan2-data-model/JSON_Schemas/v1.1.0/HTAN.BiospecimenData-v1.1.0-schema.json`
- **Matching fields in sample:** `HTAN_BIOSPECIMEN_ID`, `HTAN_PARENT_ID`
- **Non-matching fields:** `individualID`, `tumorType` are not declared in this schema (which uses ALL_CAPS_SNAKE_CASE naming, e.g. `TUMOR_CLASSIFICATION`, not `tumorType`).

### Candidate 2 — NF-OSI BiospecimenTemplate
- **Identifier:** `https://repo-prod.prod.sagebase.org/repo/v1/schema/type/registered/org.synapse.nf-biospecimentemplate` (title: `BiospecimenTemplate`)
- **Source path:** `nf-osi/nf-metadata-dictionary/registered-json-schemas/BiospecimenTemplate.json`
- **Matching fields in sample:** `individualID`, `tumorType`
- **Non-matching fields:** `HTAN_BIOSPECIMEN_ID`, `HTAN_PARENT_ID` are not declared in this schema (which uses camelCase, e.g. `specimenID`, `parentSpecimenID`).

### Inconsistency flag
The two pairs of fields follow incompatible naming conventions (HTAN ALL_CAPS vs. NF-OSI camelCase) and originate from two different organizations' biospecimen schemas. Per the rules, I cannot pick one silently. Please clarify which schema the record is intended to conform to, or check whether the sample was assembled from two different sources.
grader_notes: Assistant correctly flagged the chimera, citing HTAN ALL_CAPS fields (HTAN_BIOSPECIMEN_ID/HTAN_PARENT_ID) versus NF-OSI camelCase fields (individualID/tumorType).
